warnings: Item 19 discloses only a single franchised outlet (The Villages, FL) with 1-year data; 42 of 43 franchised outlets were excluded for being open less than 12 months, making the sample size extremely small and potentially unrepresentative.
No auditor name or audit opinion details are visible in the provided pages; financialCondition audit fields set to unknown/null.

extracted.brandBackground: Puddle Pool Services USA, Inc. was incorporated in Wyoming on September 29, 2021, and began offering U.S. franchises on January 24, 2023; it is affiliated with Puddle Pool Services Ltd. (Canada, est. 2018) and Feels Like Friday Service Brands, Inc., which owns the marks and provides proprietary CRM software.
extracted.conceptRationale: Mobile, territory-based residential and commercial pool, spa, hot tub, and water-feature cleaning and maintenance service — a home/trade services model with no retail storefront.
extracted.staffingRationale: Franchisee may appoint a non-owner general manager and hire technicians but can operate from a home office; Item 15 does not require the owner's full-time on-site presence.

leadership.name: Mark Amery
leadership.background: Founder and CEO since July 2018, with 22+ years building and franchising home-service brands.
leadership.whyItMatters: Sole decision-maker and primary trainer; short tenure of U.S. entity increases key-person risk.

leadership.name: Steff Shields
leadership.role: Franchise Partner Head of Coaching (Pre/Post Onboarding)
leadership.background: Pre- and post-onboarding specialist since January 2012.
leadership.whyItMatters: Primary franchisee support contact for launch and ongoing coaching.

leadership.name: Tyler Nerada
leadership.background: CMO and leadership team member since March 2012.
leadership.whyItMatters: Controls all in-house marketing and franchisee advertising spend.

leadership.name: Ryan Purita
leadership.background: CTO since March 2022; previously Senior Forensic Analyst at Sherlock Forensics (2008–2023).
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees proprietary PuddlePower CRM and technology fee structure.

leadership.name: Michael Terry
leadership.background: With Puddle Pool since January 2022; prior Head Pool Technician at Pools Plus (2021) and plumber at Milani's Plumbing (2018–2020).
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads field training and operational standards for franchisees.

cohorts.label: The Villages FL Franchised Outlet – Gross Revenue
cohorts.basis: Single franchised outlet in The Villages, FL; January 1 – December 31, 2025; total income per P&L reported to franchisor.

cohorts.label: The Villages FL Franchised Outlet – Net Income
cohorts.basis: Net income after all expenses and other expenses for the same single franchised outlet, January 1 – December 31, 2025.

item19.notes: 42 of 43 franchised outlets were excluded because they had been open less than 12 months; 1 corporate (California) outlet was not included in Item 19 disclosures. The single reported outlet had an unusually large Route Purchase/Acquisition expense of $225,000 which inflates costs and may not be typical. Item 19 notes figures exclude initial franchise fees, build-out/equipment costs, and owner compensation.

lineItems.notes: Single territory up to 10,000 households with pools; increases $5.00 per pool over 10,000. Item 7, p.9
Travel, lodging, and meals for franchisee to attend initial training in Florida or California. Item 7, p.9
Required grand-opening marketing blitz for first territory; paid to franchisor. Item 7, p.9

lineItems.category: Initial Inventory of Tools, Equipment and Chemicals
lineItems.notes: Must be purchased from franchisor prior to opening; covers first 3 months of supplies. Item 7, p.9
Paid to government agencies before opening. Item 7, p.10
Optional add-on equipment; paid to suppliers. Item 7, p.10

lineItems.category: Pressure Washing/Surface Cleaning and Soft Washing (optional)
lineItems.notes: Optional add-on equipment; paid to suppliers. Item 7, p.10
Computer, mobile devices, and other required technology. Item 7, p.10
Required CRM system setup cost; paid to suppliers before opening. Item 7, p.10
Estimate for leasing a brand-standard vehicle; paid to vendor/lessor/lender. Item 7, p.10
Branded vehicle wrap; paid to suppliers. Item 7, p.10
Legal, accounting, and entity formation fees; paid to professional service providers. Item 7, p.10

lineItems.category: Uniform, Business Card, Other Physical Marketing Materials
lineItems.notes: Required branded materials; paid to suppliers. Item 7, p.10
First quarterly premium for required minimum insurance coverage; paid to insurer. Item 7, p.10
Working capital for first 3 months including rent, utilities, payroll, royalties, and miscellaneous; does not include owner compensation or debt service. Item 7, p.10-11

hiddenCosts.name: Brand Development Fund Contribution (if established)
hiddenCosts.description: 2% of monthly gross revenue (up to 3%) payable to the Brand Development Fund once established; currently not active.

hiddenCosts.name: Late Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $75 per late royalty, brand fund, or gross revenue report submission.

hiddenCosts.name: Interest on Overdue Amounts
hiddenCosts.description: 18% per annum or highest lawful rate on any unpaid balance.

hiddenCosts.name: Non-Sufficient Funds Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $250 per returned check or denied ACH transfer.

hiddenCosts.name: Successor Agreement Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 10% of then-current initial franchise fee due at renewal.

hiddenCosts.name: Transfer Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 10% of then-current initial franchise fee for most transfers; $1,500 for intra-entity or family transfers.

hiddenCosts.name: Additional Trainee Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $1,000 per additional person attending initial training beyond the first three.

hiddenCosts.name: Remedial Training Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisor's then-current per-diem rate plus travel expenses for in-territory remedial training.

hiddenCosts.name: Audit/Examination of Books Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Cost of audit plus related expenses if gross revenue understated by 2% or more.

hiddenCosts.name: Customer Dispute Resolution Reimbursement
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must reimburse franchisor for any refunds issued to customers on franchisee's behalf within 10 days.

hiddenCosts.name: Insurance Reimbursement + 10% Admin Fee
hiddenCosts.description: If franchisee fails to maintain required insurance, franchisor may procure it and charge franchisee cost plus 10% administrative fee.

hiddenCosts.name: Liquidated Damages on Default Termination
hiddenCosts.description: Greater of $50,000 or up to 24 months of rolling 12-month average royalty fees and brand fund contributions.

hiddenCosts.name: Relocation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $5,000 payable to franchisor if franchisee is approved to relocate the franchised business.

hiddenCosts.name: Ongoing Vehicle Lease Payments
hiddenCosts.description: Estimated $1,800–$3,200 per month for required branded vehicle lease beyond initial period.

hiddenCosts.name: State Licensing and Certification Fees
hiddenCosts.description: Some states require pool/spa technician licenses or certifications with associated fees.

operationalRisks.title: General Financial Condition of Franchisor
operationalRisks.description: The Special Risks section explicitly states the franchisor's financial condition calls into question its ability to provide services and support to franchisees.

operationalRisks.title: Short Operating History
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor incorporated in 2021 and began franchising in January 2023, giving it a very limited track record.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Royalty and Advertising Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee must pay minimum royalty ($600/month) and local advertising fees regardless of sales level; failure can result in termination.

operationalRisks.title: Spouse Liability Guarantee
operationalRisks.description: Married franchisees must have their spouse sign a personal guaranty making the spouse liable for all financial obligations under the franchise agreement.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution (Wyoming)
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be mediated and litigated in Wyoming, which may disadvantage franchisees located in other states.

operationalRisks.title: Extremely Small Item 19 Sample
operationalRisks.description: Only 1 of 44 outlets is reported in Item 19; 42 franchised outlets were excluded for being open less than 12 months, making financial performance data highly unreliable.

operationalRisks.title: Rapid System Growth with Unproven Franchisees
operationalRisks.description: 43 new franchised outlets opened in 2025 alone, with 17 additional agreements signed but not yet open, creating significant execution and support risk for a young franchisor.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Controls All Marketing and Social Media
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees cannot create their own social media profiles or advertising; all marketing is conducted in-house by franchisor, limiting franchisee autonomy.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Owns All Customer Data
operationalRisks.description: All customer data collected by franchisee is owned exclusively by franchisor and the license to use it terminates immediately upon agreement expiration or termination.

operationalRisks.title: Non-Competition Covenant (24 months / 25 miles post-termination)
operationalRisks.description: After termination or expiration, franchisee cannot operate a competing pool cleaning business within 25 miles of the territory or any Puddle Pool Services location for 24 months.

operationalRisks.title: Liquidated Damages for Confidentiality/Non-Compete Violation
operationalRisks.description: $100,000 per violation plus attorney's fees if franchisee breaches confidentiality or non-competition covenants.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor May Change System Standards Unilaterally
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor can modify the operations manual and system standards at any time without franchisee consent, potentially requiring additional investment.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p.9-11 — franchise operates from a home-based or shared office; no commercial lease rent figure is disclosed in Item 7.

riskReasons: 4 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisor financial distress disclosed (negative net worth) — confirm its ability to support the system against the audited statements.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.
No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $118,750 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion.

insights.conceptRationale: Mobile, territory-based residential and commercial pool, spa, hot tub, and water-feature cleaning and maintenance service — a home/trade services model with no retail storefront.

benchmark.label: Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Crew utilization, callback/warranty cost, and customer-acquisition cost. Low fixed overhead, but CAC and scheduling efficiency decide profitability.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–9 months to a steady job pipeline.
considerations: Budget materials/subcontractor COGS ~25–40% and field labor ~20–35%.
CAC (lead cost per booked job) is the dominant hidden line — ask Item 20 operators what they actually spend per job.
Confirm territory size, lead-generation expectations, and any required call-center/marketing fees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 10–21% — the 20–35% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
financialCondition.context: Worth perspective: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt; revenue is growing; positive net worth; the business is profitable. Early-stage franchisors commonly run losses and carry a deficit while investing to scale — this reads more like spending ahead of revenue than a failing business. The real question is runway: how the gap is funded, and for how long.
body: What we found (2025-12-31, audited by DA Advisory Group PLLC): Net income of $752K on $3.40M of revenue — roughly 0.8x spent per $1 earned. Positive net worth of $575K. Cash of $836K against $1.30M of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 2.23). Note: about 93% of those liabilities is deferred revenue — prepaid franchise fees the company owes as future services, not borrowed money. Netting it out, liabilities are still about 0.1x assets.
Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
